title: Mirabilis grayana
taxonomy: Mirabilis grayana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Polygonaceae. It is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States. The genus Mirabilis is part of the same family and contains several species of similar plants. Mirabilis grayana is often referred to as the Gray's four o'clock flower. The classification of Mirabilis grayana reflects its unique characteristics and relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Mirabilis grayana, also known as Gray's four o'clock, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, characterized by its bright pink to purple flowers and edible, starchy roots. The plant has been traditionally used for its various health benefits, including treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as supporting the immune system and reducing inflammation.
